title: Refactor Codex capability reporting
description: Refactor Codex client capability modeling so init output can distinguish Codex official support from Fabric-installed state, and update the init summary / reason message behavior accordingly without changing hook installation yet.
test: Update existing init capability summary coverage so interactive Codex output no longer asserts generic manual-only wording; verify current config-install Codex TOML path still passes.
acceptance_criteria: Codex rows in init capability summary no longer imply Codex lacks skill/hook support; reason/follow-up messaging reflects partial-install state instead of unsupported/manual-only state; existing Codex MCP config behavior remains unchanged.
hints: Prefer additive status modeling over ad-hoc string special-casing || packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-mcp-scope.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/config-install.test.ts
prev_context:
[Explore capabilities] `detectClientSupports()` models Codex CLI as supported/detected when `~/.codex` exists or `clientPaths.codexCLI` is set, but its capability map is fixed to `bootstrap=true,mcp=true,hook=false,skill=false`; this means init always renders Codex as `Skill=manual` and `Follow-up=manual step required`, regardless of installed MCP config (`packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts`). `formatInitReasonMessage()` similarly switches to the manual-body unless any detected client has `skill=true`, so Codex-only setups get manual follow-up messaging. i18n already carries both summary headers/statuses in `en` and `zh-CN`, and regression coverage exists in `init-mcp-scope.test.ts` for interactive Codex output plus `config-install.test.ts` for TOML MCP install.
  Key files: packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-mcp-scope.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/config-install.test.ts
[Explore testing] Current minimal regression suite can stay small and reuse existing fixture helpers in packages/cli/__tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts. Evidence: install behavior is covered by init-claude-install.test.ts (writes skill/hook/settings/bootstrap and hook gating); capability summary for Codex is already asserted in init-mcp-scope.test.ts via initCommand.run with TTY+console mocks; non-destructive semantics are isolated in init-nondestructive.test.ts and the regression case in init-force.test.ts. Safest phase-1 additions are to extend those files rather than add new harnesses.
  Key files: packages/cli/__tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-claude-install.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-mcp-scope.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-nondestructive.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/init-force.test.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts
